rationale.marketplace_relevance: Directly concerns how the dominant UK portal prices access to its marketplace — core to the classifieds/portal business model.
rationale.event_scale: £1.5B with 4,000 claimants is the largest legal action against a property portal on record. Industry-defining scale.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Avito charges agents subscription fees. The 'pricing power vs. agent backlash' dynamic is structurally identical. High portability.
rationale.urgency: Case will develop over 3-5 years but the filing itself sets a market-level precedent. Immediate awareness required.
rationale.novelty: First class-action of this scale against a property portal in Europe.

scoring_notes: High-confidence case. All annotators likely to agree. The legal framing doesn't reduce relevance — the underlying signal is about portal pricing power.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Core product redesign of a top-3 US portal around AI — the primary search interface, the most visited page on any portal.
rationale.event_scale: Realtor.com with 68M monthly visitors is one of the three most visited property portals globally.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Conversational/AI search replaces filter-based search — directly applicable to Avito's search product. The behavioural model transfers.
rationale.urgency: Product is live in production, not a demo. Competitors (including Avito) need to respond or research immediately.
rationale.novelty: Full AI-first redesign with filter panel removal, not just an AI chatbot added on top. Structural novelty.

scoring_notes: This case produced consensus among all annotators. The score gap to jtbd08-003 (CoStar) is marginal — annotators may differ by ±3 on individual dimensions.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: AI search as primary discovery mechanism on Homes.com — same class of event as jtbd08-002.
rationale.event_scale: Homes.com is growing from 8M to 30M monthly visitors — scale is significant and increasing.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Same AI search portability argument as Realtor.com. CoStar data advantage angle is also transferable.
rationale.urgency: Slightly lower than jtbd08-002 because this launched in February — some elapsed time reduces urgency.
rationale.novelty: CoStar's data moat angle adds a differentiated novelty dimension — not just another chatbot.

scoring_notes: Score virtually identical to jtbd08-002 — both are tier-1 AI search launches. Annotator consensus should be high.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: M&A in transaction infrastructure is adjacent to marketplace model — relevant but one step removed.
rationale.event_scale: $150M acquisition by a significant player (Opendoor). Meaningful but not dominant-player scale.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Escrow and title insurance are US-specific legal infrastructure. Not directly portable to Russia/CIS markets. Concept of 'transaction-layer monetisation' is portable; specific technology is not.
rationale.urgency: Strategic direction confirmed but 12-month integration timeline reduces immediate urgency.
rationale.novelty: iBuying pivot to transaction services infrastructure is a strategically novel shift in Opendoor's business model.

scoring_notes: Key annotator discussion point: portability_to_avito. US title insurance is specific to US property law. If scoring 'business model portability' rather than 'technical portability', score rises to 7. Used 5 here for technical portability to reflect the constraint.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Direct regulatory action on portal subscription pricing — highly relevant to portal business model and monetization strategy.
rationale.event_scale: Czech market is small (~10M population, limited direct commercial relevance). Scale capped accordingly.
rationale.portability_to_avito: The regulatory mechanism (competition law forcing price cuts on dominant portal) is portable as a risk scenario for any dominant marketplace. Avito's dominance in Russian classifieds makes this particularly relevant.
rationale.urgency: 90-day compliance window means this evolves slowly. Monitoring cadence sufficient.
rationale.novelty: First EU competition law action compelling a property portal to cut subscription prices. High novelty as regulatory precedent.

scoring_notes: The tension between low event_scale (Czech market size) and high novelty/portability produces a medium-priority case that illustrates why multi-dimensional scoring is necessary. Simple 'is this big?' question would undervalue it.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Pre-market listing type changes how inventory flows through the portal — affects core supply-side dynamics.
rationale.event_scale: Zillow with 220M monthly visitors. Brokerage adoption (60+ firms in 3 weeks) confirms scale.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Pre-market/coming-soon listing category is applicable to any property portal. Avito could implement similar feature.
rationale.urgency: 60+ brokerages signed in 3 weeks — adoption is fast. Monitoring and product team response warranted.
rationale.novelty: First major portal to formalise and scale a pre-market listing category with explicit MLS exclusivity mechanism.

scoring_notes: This sits just above the digest threshold. Some annotators may score event_scale lower (7) if weighting by direct US/global market impact vs. Avito relevance. Total score range likely 72-80.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Agent tooling is secondary to core marketplace/portal product. Relevant but one level removed.
rationale.event_scale: Lofty is a mid-size CRM vendor, not a top-tier portal. Market impact is limited to agent tech adoption.
rationale.portability_to_avito: AI CRM for agents is applicable to Avito's agent product strategy. Portability is reasonable but indirect.
rationale.urgency: No time-sensitive competitive threat. AI CRM space is developing gradually.
rationale.novelty: AI-powered pipeline automation in a CRM is emerging as a category — novelty is moderate, not pioneering.

scoring_notes: Clear weak signal territory. The $499/month price point and 40% time saving claim are data points worth tracking but not digest-worthy.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Inter-portal competitive dynamics and metric disputes are relevant for understanding market structure.
rationale.event_scale: French-only dispute. Limited direct global significance.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Market share disputes and competing measurement methodologies occur in any portal market. Concept is portable, specifics are not.
rationale.urgency: Ongoing background rivalry — no acute event requiring immediate response.
rationale.novelty: Portals disputing each other's traffic metrics is not a new phenomenon.

scoring_notes: Just above the 40-55 weak signal boundary due to marketplace_relevance score. Could go either way on whether to include in weak signals section.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Behavioural data showing buyers switching to AI search is a leading indicator of where portal investment should go.
rationale.event_scale: 2,400-respondent nationally representative US survey is statistically credible. 38% adoption rate is a market-moving data point.
rationale.portability_to_avito: AI search adoption behavioural data is transferable — the adoption pattern will emerge in Russian market with some lag. Provides benchmarks for planning.
rationale.urgency: Survey data establishes current state but doesn't require immediate product response — planning horizon signal.
rationale.novelty: First large-sample study quantifying AI search adoption in residential real estate. Establishes a benchmark.

scoring_notes: Annotator discussion: is behavioural survey data 'news' or 'context'? Consensus: it's a significant data point that elevates the urgency for AI search product investment — medium priority is right.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Classifieds portal moving into full transaction execution is a business model shift relevant to any marketplace.
rationale.event_scale: Dubai market is significant for MENA but small globally. 200 transactions in pilot is modest.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Avito as classifieds platform considering deeper transaction integration — this is a direct strategic analogue. AED 15,000 flat fee vs. percentage-based model is a valuable pricing data point.
rationale.urgency: Pilot just completed. Early-stage model — 12-18 month horizon for maturation.
rationale.novelty: Flat transaction fee replacing percentage-based commission in a classifieds-to-transactions pivot is a genuinely novel monetisation model.

scoring_notes: High portability_to_avito keeps this at medium despite small market scale. The pricing model innovation is the most analytically valuable element.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Rental market data is useful context but does not signal a product or business model change.
rationale.event_scale: US national rental data is statistically significant for the US market but not globally directive.
rationale.portability_to_avito: US rental market dynamics are structurally different from Russia/CIS. Supply surplus story is US-specific.
rationale.novelty: Rent deceleration is a well-established macro trend, not a new discovery.

scoring_notes: This is right at the borderline of weak signals (40-55) and irrelevant (<40). A case for including as a 'macro context' weak signal. Annotators may differ ±5 on event_scale.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: M&A consolidation in European portal market is relevant to understanding competitive dynamics.
rationale.event_scale: €620M acquisition of two top-3 portals in a major European market is a significant consolidation event.
rationale.portability_to_avito: European portal consolidation trend is relevant as context — doesn't directly affect Avito's operating environment.
rationale.urgency: Q3 close timeline — medium-term development to monitor.
rationale.novelty: Scout24 expanding beyond DACH region into Southern Europe is noteworthy geographic diversification.

scoring_notes: Straightforward medium priority case. Score range expected 58-68 among annotators.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Premium ad format with brand presence elements is core to marketplace monetisation evolution.
rationale.event_scale: Avito's Jobs vertical is large and the data (240 employers, 2.1x CTR) provides real performance benchmarks.
rationale.portability_to_avito: This IS Avito. portability_to_avito = 10 — it's a direct internal case study with data directly applicable to Real Estate vertical.
rationale.urgency: Live product with 30-day performance data. Real Estate team can act on this immediately.
rationale.novelty: Moderate — branded formats exist on other platforms, but the internal performance data is novel and proprietary.

scoring_notes: portability_to_avito=10 is exceptional and intentional — this is an internal Avito product with direct data for the Real Estate vertical. Annotators should not downgrade this due to it being 'internal'.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Fundraise with a stated focus on verified listings and AI valuation is relevant product strategy signal.
rationale.event_scale: $400M is large for MENA proptech but MENA is not a top-priority market.
rationale.portability_to_avito: Verified listings quality infrastructure and AI valuation are directly relevant to Avito's product roadmap.
rationale.urgency: Capital raise with investment over 2-3 years — low urgency.
rationale.novelty: The 'depth over expansion' strategic framing at $400M scale is a notable market signal about MENA market maturation.

scoring_notes: This sits at the borderline. The verified listings investment angle may push some annotators toward 62-65. The MENA geography caps it. Use this as a calibration case.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Self-serve advertising analytics is a core capability for any ad-supported marketplace.
rationale.event_scale: Avito is a top-3 classifieds platform in Russia — the scale of the rollout is significant within its market.
rationale.portability_to_avito: This IS Avito Adtech's own product — portability_to_avito=10. Real Estate is the primary identified beneficiary.
rationale.urgency: Product is live. Real Estate team should evaluate adoption strategy and integration with developer/agent accounts.
rationale.novelty: Self-serve analytics with impression-level data has been available on major ad platforms for years. Moderate novelty within Avito's product set.

scoring_notes: Second Avito-internal case (alongside jtbd08-013). Both get portability_to_avito=10. Annotators should understand that 'portability' includes 'directly applicable internal product data'.

rationale.marketplace_relevance: Real estate as a native vertical inside a generalist AI search engine is a direct threat to standalone classifieds portals — including Avito Real Estate. This models the 'portal disintermediation' risk: buyers never leave the search engine. Highly relevant to marketplace business model.
rationale.event_scale: Yandex is the dominant search engine in Russia with 60%+ search market share and operates in the same country as Avito. Q4 revenue growth of 28% and real estate session depth +31% are credible large-scale signals from a top-3 Russian tech company.
rationale.portability_to_avito: HIGHEST AVITO RELEVANCE in this benchmark set. Yandex explicitly names Avito Real Estate as a competitive target. The 'AI search as portal replacement' model is the exact competitive threat Avito Real Estate must monitor. CPCs up 19% shows monetisation is working — the model is viable, not just experimental.
rationale.urgency: Active competitive dynamic with Q4 metrics showing it is working now, not a future threat. However, full disruption to Avito's listing volume would take time — urgency is elevated but not immediate-crisis level.
rationale.novelty: Integrating real estate listings as a native AI search answer (not a link to a portal) is a new distribution model for property. It has been discussed but Yandex showing real monetisation data is the first validated instance of it working at scale.

scoring_notes: This is the highest portability_to_avito score outside of Avito-internal articles. Evaluators should verify the model correctly identifies the Avito-specific competitive relevance and does not default to treating Yandex as a 'foreign market' low-relevance case. Formula check: (8×0.35)+(8×0.25)+(9×0.20)+(7×0.10)+(8×0.10) = 2.80+2.00+1.80+0.70+0.80 = 8.10 × 10 = 81 (rounded to 82 with annotator judgment on +1 for strategic primacy).
benchmark_note: CALIBRATION CASE: portability_to_avito=9 is intentional. A model that scores this as portability_to_avito=5 because 'Yandex is a different company' misunderstands the scoring dimension — portability measures applicability of the pattern to Avito, not whether Avito is mentioned. The Yandex AI-search-as-portal model is directly applicable as a competitive threat model.
